translated: [CORNER: Story Watchalong] All right, I've picked out some scenes that I personally wanted to talk about,
so let's take a look at them together.
Here they are.
Wonderful.
Such a good story.
It's a really good story.
That's how I feel about it.
Since I'm someone who has already chosen my own career path,
witnessing the moment someone else makes a choice—
even if it's just through an event story—really resonates with me.
There are always moments where you wonder if you're making the right choice. But if 10 or 20 years from now,
you can look back and think, "I'm glad I made that choice back then," then it was definitely worth it.
I think that feeling comes from the effort you put in. When it comes to things that require hard work,
I don't think there's really a "right" or "wrong" choice.
With a choice that involves real effort, you might think a year or two later,
"Oh, I shouldn't have done this."
But looking at it over 10 or 20 years, I'm sure there are times
where it all connects in the end.
You'll think, "If I hadn't done that then, I wouldn't be able to do this now."
Even if you're in a different profession, I think you'll feel that way.
So for any kids out there who are working hard or have something they want to work toward, please keep at it.
I hope this event story serves as a catalyst for you to make that decision for yourself.
[CORNER: Card Illustrations] Now then, let's talk about the illustrations.
[CORNER: Fan Letter Corner] First, let me read a letter.
This one is from Meshi-san. Thanks for writing in.
Thank you very much.
"Hello, Sato-san. Kon-Niigo!"
"I'm always having fun playing ProSeka."
"The 4-star illustrations this time, especially Mizuki's, were so good that I went all out on the gacha to get them."
"Just like the trained illustrations for 'Secret Distance' and 'Where Shall This Path Lead?',"
"Where?"
"Where Shall This Path Lead?"
"Mizuki was shown in a space filled with torsos."
Torsos appear quite often in Mizuki's illustrations.
They're like mannequins for displaying clothes.
Like the one right here... here... here... oh, here!
Right here.
This is called a torso.
It's the torso from "Reaching Out for an Unraveling Tomorrow."
"It was an illustration of Mizuki in a space with torsos."
"Usually, the torso space represents Mizuki's dark feelings,"
"but this time, after feeling the warmth of family and friends, they're looking forward."
"The space now represents Mizuki's bright and cheerful feelings."
"I was so happy to see this change in Mizuki's state of mind."
"I can't thank the staff enough for creating such wonderful illustrations and scenarios,"
"and the voice actors for their amazing performances that bring such depth to the characters."
"Thank you for always creating such wonderful events."
"Please take care of yourselves and keep up the good work."
"I'll be rooting for you as a fellow Tofu."
That's what they wrote.
Thank you, Meshi-san.
Yeah, the colors in the illustrations are quite rare for Niigo, aren't they?
We've had pale colors before, but seeing Niigo bathed in sunlight...
I mean, they don't go outside much to begin with,
so seeing Niigo during the day felt very fresh to me.
Usually, we don't see this kind of touch very often.
But everyone's cards were great, not just Mizuki's—both before and after training.
It made me realize what amazing artists we have.
I'm truly grateful. And actually,
we've received comments from the illustration team regarding the outfits this time,
so I'd like to share those as well.
Oh, there's a comment about it too.
Someone said, "Pure cuteness is rare, isn't it?"
"Mizuki, having been accepted by Niigo, wants to create clothes that can help children who have similar worries,"
"or children who can't bring themselves to like who they are."
"Inspired by the story of Mizuki thinking about a future career in fashion,"
"we wanted to express the image of the future they're starting to envision—a future that's finally coming into view."
"So, we went with a simple and clean one-piece design with fewer gothic elements."
"As a special touch, we included the yellow daisy embroidery used in Mizuki's previous unit outfit."
"This carries the message of 'being true to yourself,'"
"resulting in a simple outfit design that is uniquely Mizuki."
"Also, to reflect the song's calm, warm impression and Mizuki's faint expectations for the future,"
"we used a palette of soft, gentle colors."
That's what they said.
I see.
It's true that the colors are soft for everyone across the board.
So that's why.
Did everyone notice the daisies? They're in there!
The daisies are there.
Oh, I see people commenting "Daisy, daisy" now.
